The correct answer according to the World Health Organization (WHO) definition for identifying a high-endemic area for meningococcal meningitis is:
\boxed{C} Based on the retrieved articles, particularly the statement from "[article-24976_26]", which specifies that "areas with high (>10 cases per 100,000)" endemicity require large-scale meningococcal vaccine programs, along with consistent references indicating that higher incidences define high-endemic areas, option C (\>10 per 1,00,000 population) aligns best with the WHO classification.
